title: Cephalomanes apiifolium
taxonomy: Cephalomanes apiifolium is a species of fern in the family Hymenophyllaceae. It belongs to the kingdom Plantae and the phylum Marchantiophyta. The genus Cephalomanes is part of the division Polypodiophyta and the class Polypodiopsida. This species is native to tropical regions of Asia and is often found in shaded, humid environments. Cephalomanes apiifolium is an epiphytic fern that grows on tree trunks and branches.
uses: Cephalomanes apiifolium is traditionally used in folk medicine in Southeast Asia. It is believed to possess anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of conditions such as arthritis and joint pain. The plant's leaves are often used to treat fever and various respiratory issues, including bronchitis and asthma. In some cultures, Cephalomanes apiifolium is also used to treat skin conditions like eczema and dermatitis. Further research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal properties and potential applications.

Traditionally, Cephalomanes apiifolium has been used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, skin conditions, and inflammation, due to its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties.
